name: raphson-mp
documentation:
# Raphson Music Player

Web-based music player for communities (hacker spaces, maker spaces).

## Introduction

Music is crucial when working on projects. However, when you're with a group, who is in charge? Everyone should be, of course! This music player allows everyone to create their own playlists. Then, when you're together, enable the playlists of everyone who is present. Music will be chosen from each playlist with a fair distribution.

## Features

- No repetition
    - Shuffle algorithm ensures the least recently played songs are picked first.
- No metadata hassle
    - Album covers are automatically fetched from MusicBrainz or using a web scraper
    - Lyrics are automatically fetched using a web scraper
    - Metadata editor to easily correct metadata on the fly
    - Audio is loudness-normalized ensuring consistent volume for all genres.
- Easy file management
    - Built-in web file browser to download, upload, rename and delete files
    - Built-in music downloader using `yt-dlp`
- Responsive and mobile compatible
    - Touch-friendly interface
    - Audio files and images are compressed on-the-fly, with various quality options to save data.
    - Also keyboard-friendly interface; hotkeys are available for common actions, allowing you to skip a track while wearing welding gloves.
- Statistics
    - See what others are playing now or in the past
    - Statistics page with graphs based on historical data
    - Last.fm scrobbling (each user can connect their own last.fm account)
- Fun
    - Enable 'Album cover meme mode' to replace album covers by (sometimes) funny memes related to the song title.
- News
    - Optionally, insert news from [news scraper](https://github.com/Derkades/news-scraper) on the hour to keep up-to-date.
- Minimal: fast and secure
    - Written in pure HTML, CSS and JavaScript with minimal third party libraries. The frontend should be fast, even on an old laptop or cheap single board computer.
    - Queued songs are stored as blobs in the browser's storage. Temporary network connection issues are no problem.
    - Python dependencies are kept to a minimum, and we ship a custom build of FFmpeg for minimal storage usage.
    - Very strict Content-Security-Policy allows no communication from clients to the internet.

## Screenshots

See [docs/screenshots.md](docs/screenshots.md).

## Usage

See [docs/installation.md](docs/installation.md).

## User management

See [docs/manage.md](docs/manage.md).

## Development and translation

See [docs/development.md](docs/development.md).

          SELECT column, COUNT(*) GROUP BY column
Returns: series_dict for chart() function

functions.name: raphson_mp.charts.rows_to_xy_multi
documentation:
Convert rows
[a1, b1, c1]
[a1, b2, c2]
[a2, b1, c3]
to xdata: [a1, a2], ydata: {b1: [c1, c3], b2: [c2, 0]}
Where a appears on the x axis, b appears in in the legend (is stacked), and c is data.

functions.name: raphson_mp.routes.activity.route_now_playing
documentation:
Send info about currently playing track. Sent frequently by the music player.
POST body should contain a json object with:
 - csrf (str): CSRF token
 - track (str): Track relpath
 - paused (bool): Whether track is paused
 - progress (int): Track position, as a percentage

functions.name: raphson_mp.routes.activity.route_played
documentation:
Route to submit an entry to played tracks history, optionally also
scrobbling to last.fm. Used by web music player and also by offline
sync to submit many previously played tracks.
POST body:
 - track: relpath
 - timestamp: time at which track met played conditions (roughly)
 - csrf: csrf token (ignored in offline mode)

functions.name: raphson_mp.cache.store
documentation:
Args:
    key: Cache key
    data: Data to cache
    duration: Suggested cache duration in seconds. Cache duration is varied by up to 25%, to
              avoid high load when cache entries all expire at roughly the same time.

functions.name: raphson_mp.cache.retrieve
documentation:
Retrieve object from cache
Args:
    key: Cache key
    partial: Return partial data in the specified range (start, length)
    return_expired: Whether to return the object from cache even when expired, but not cleaned
                    up yet. Should be set to False for short lived cache objects.

documentation: Remove any cache entries that are beyond their expire time.

documentation: Dump dict as json, encode as utf-8 and then use store()

classes.name: raphson_mp.db.ClosingConnection

methods.name: raphson_mp.db.ClosingConnection.__exit__
documentation:
Strangely (in my opinion), the sqlite connection is not closed by the standard context manager.
So, we wrap the standard __exit__ with a "proper" __exit__ that calls close()
See: https://docs.python.org/3/library/sqlite3.html#sqlite3-connection-context-manager
Context manager for SQLite that manages closing the connection
The context manager of the real sqlite connection is for transactions instead

documentation:
observe_changes() and restart() from Quart. observe_changes() was modified to

Verify session token, and return corresponding user

Returns: User object if session token is valid, or None if invalid

functions.name: raphson_mp.auth.verify_auth_cookie
documentation:
Verify auth token sent as cookie, raising AuthError if missing or not valid.
Args:
    conn: Read-only database connection
    require_admin: Whether logging in as a non-admin account should be treated as an authentication failure
    redirect_to_login: Whether the user should sent a redirect if authentication failed, instead
                       of showing a 403 page. Should be set to True for pages, and to False for API endpoints.

methods.name: raphson_mp.i18n.LazyString.__init__
documentation:
Constract a Lazy String.

Arguments:
    func: The function to use for the string.
    args: Arguments for the function.
    kwargs: Kwargs for the function.

The `LazyString` class provides the ability to declare
translations without app context. The translations don't
happen until they are actually needed.

classes.name: raphson_mp.music.AudioType

class_variables.name: raphson_mp.music.AudioType.WEBM_OPUS_HIGH
documentation:
Opus audio in WebM container, for music player streaming with lower data
usage.

class_variables.name: raphson_mp.music.AudioType.WEBM_OPUS_LOW
documentation:
Special AAC audio in MP4 container for Safari. Apple press releases
say Opus in WebM are supported, but that does not seem to be the case.
caniuse.com claims Opus in CAF container is supported, but doesn't seem
to work either (tested using Safari 16.0 in macOS Monterey VM).
We have to use the less-efficient AAC codec at a higher bit rate.

class_variables.name: raphson_mp.music.AudioType.MP4_AAC
documentation:
MP3 files with metadata (including cover art), for use with external
music player applications and devices Uses the MP3 format for broadest
compatibility.
Opus audio in WebM container, for music player streaming.

methods.name: raphson_mp.music.Track.playlist
documentation: Returns: Part of track path before first slash

methods.name: raphson_mp.music.Track.metadata
documentation: Returns: Cached metadata for this track, as a Metadata object

methods.name: raphson_mp.music.Track.get_cover
documentation:
Find album cover using MusicBrainz or Bing.
Parameters:
    meta: Track metadata
Returns: Album cover image bytes, or None if MusicBrainz nor bing found an image.
